pages.raw_text:
"The government has taken an in
terest in 1t and the agricultural de-
partment has agreed to send out
lectures to eXpound upon apple cul-
ture and shipping. There will be
sent from all parts of the country
about 81 carloads of apples, which
will give you some impression of
the size of the show. For the ex-
hibit we have secured the Billy
Sunday tabernacle, which is to be
built this month.


ALREADY about $4,000 has been
contributed to the enterprise by
private subscription. some of the
donors giving more than we now
ask of all the people in the county
combined. At first about $4,000
was secured, and later when it was
found necessary additional funds
were called for and received also
from private sources.


"The railroads have granted, a
special rate to remain during the
show of less than one and third
fares for the round trip"


Gordon Corbaley addressed the
commissioners and stated that the
show was exciting national interest
and to the people Of this city it is
perhaps of more importance than
the freight rate case.
